name: Stripe
description: Clean SaaS reference — radiant purple-to-cyan gradient depth over crisp white, narrow display type, generous whitespace. The system whispers technical credibility, never shouts.

--color-cloud-whisper.role: Primary surface — page background, card fills, hero base layer

--color-midnight-ink.role: Primary text + dark sections — navy that reads as black but warms in mass

--color-cobalt-spark.role: Signature accent — CTA buttons, key links, gradient origin

--color-lagoon-spark.role: Gradient terminus — paired with cobalt-spark for hero washes only
--color-lagoon-spark.context: vivid cyan; never appears alone, only as gradient partner

--color-slate-body.role: Secondary body text on white surfaces
--color-slate-body.context: muted slate, calibrated for long-read paragraphs

--color-fog-divider.context: near-white with a hint of cool

--color-mist-surface.role: Secondary section background — alternating bands, code-block bg
--color-mist-surface.context: barely-blue tint to separate sections without weight

--font-display-sohne.role: Display headlines + nav — narrow, geometric, technical-confident

--font-mono-source.role: Code samples, API references, inline tokens

antiPatterns: Never apply --color-cobalt-spark to large surface fills — it loses its CTA pulling power past 15% of viewport. Reserve for buttons, key links, and gradient anchors only.
Never use --color-cobalt-spark and --color-lagoon-spark as solid blocks side-by-side — they exist as gradient partners. Solid blocks read as 1998 GeoCities.
Body text must NOT use --color-midnight-ink on white — Stripe uses --color-slate-body for paragraphs to soften contrast on long reads. Reserve midnight-ink for headlines and dark surfaces.
Never set --color-cloud-whisper backgrounds with shadow heavier than 4px blur — Stripe shadows are nearly invisible, communicating depth through hue not weight.
